title: Fix trade preview zero-cost rounding
description: Trade cost preview on oracle detail pages always shows 0 DUSDC for quantity 1 and can understate small orders because the UI calls predict::get_trade_amounts with TRADE_PREVIEW_UNIT_QUANTITY = 1n, then scales the returned values client-side. On-chain devInspect for oracle 0xb79524498a9947307e192d8045772150dc47aade4f9e09bd4b6fe3236b9e3125 and strike 77,837 returned 0,0 for quantity 1, but 427602,407875 for quantity 1,000,000 and 427602835,407875355 for quantity 1,000,000,000. This proves integer rounding happens inside get_trade_amounts before client scaling. Fix by passing the user's parsedQuantity directly to getOracleTradeAmounts and removing the unit-query scaling, then verify preview amounts for quantity 1 and larger quantities.
acceptance_criteria: Oracle trade preview uses parsedQuantity in the devInspect call. Quantity 1 no longer displays a misleading 0 DUSDC when the contract returns non-zero cost at contract-sized quantities. Client-side unit scaling is removed or limited to display-only logic that cannot hide contract rounding. Tests cover the quantity passed into getOracleTradeAmounts or the scaling regression.
close_reason: fixed

Required Vitest dependencies/config are added consistently with the Vite app. The migrated test still covers manager creation, manager deposit, MarketKey construction, mint argument shape, redeem target selection, and DUSDC amount scaling.

title: Fix oracle page refresh UX and long-session stability
description: Oracle detail page refreshes quote UX too aggressively and may keep long-running network work alive after route changes. Investigate and tighten refresh lifecycles so background polling does not blank actionable quotes or accumulate stale async work.
close_reason: Implemented serialized refresh polling, abortable server fetches, stable background quote refresh UX, and chart measurement stabilization; verified with build, tests, and local browser check.

title: Implement close and redeem position flow
description: Build the user-facing redeem workflow for existing directional positions. Support live redeem and settled redeem after checking oracle lifecycle and manager position quantity. Partial close is in scope because predict::redeem accepts quantity and predict_manager::decrease_position only requires stored quantity >= redeemed quantity.
acceptance_criteria: As a trader with an open position, I can choose a full or partial quantity to close. As a trader, I see redeem payout, remaining quantity, preview countdown, and whether the payout is live-priced or settled before signing. As a trader, redeem and payout withdrawal from PredictManager happen in one wallet signature when possible. As a trader, I can redeem successfully when I own enough manager quantity and the oracle is quoteable or settled. As a trader, I get clear blocking states for insufficient quantity, expired-but-not-settled oracle, zero payout, stale preview, failed transaction, and delayed server refresh.
close_reason: Implemented wallet portfolio positions and close/redeem flow

title: Implement wallet portfolio positions for close flow
description: Add a wallet-focused positions or portfolio view backed by manager summary, manager positions summary, and current oracle state. The view should show only the connected trader's open position quantities and give the close flow the data it needs for redeem previews.
acceptance_criteria: As a connected trader, I can see my open BTC/DUSDC positions grouped by oracle, expiry, strike, and UP/DOWN side. As a connected trader, I can see quantity, estimated redeem payout, expiry, settlement status, and last refresh time. As a trader, I can filter out global historical activity and focus on my manager-owned positions. As a trader, empty and server-lag states explain whether I have no manager, no positions, or pending index refresh.
close_reason: Implemented wallet portfolio positions and close/redeem flow

title: Implement open position flow on oracle market page
description: Turn the existing oracle trade preview into a complete BTC/DUSDC position mint workflow. Reuse the strike, side, quantity, and pricing preview controls, then add wallet connection, manager readiness, balance sufficiency, submit, confirmation, and refresh states.
acceptance_criteria: As a trader viewing an active BTC oracle, I can choose UP or DOWN, strike, and quantity from valid market grid values. As a trader, I see estimated cost, manager balance impact, and stale-preview warnings before signing. As a trader with enough manager DUSDC, I can mint the selected position and see the transaction result. As a trader, I cannot submit when the oracle is not tradeable, quantity is invalid, quote balance is insufficient, ask bounds reject the trade, or trading is paused.
notes: Scope decision from risk-trading-3ec.1: open-position submit should build one PTB that deposits the required wallet DUSDC into PredictManager, then calls predict::mint for the selected BTC/DUSDC UP/DOWN MarketKey. No range support in v1. Active preview refreshes every 5s with visual countdown; block signing when preview is stale.
close_reason: Completed open-position flow hardening: quote auto-refresh, stale quote submit blocking, ask-bounds validation, and updated trading scope copy.

title: Compose inline DUSDC deposit for open-position PTB
description: Add the transaction path and UX validation for moving DUSDC from the connected wallet into the user's PredictManager inside the same programmable transaction block that mints a position. This is required because predict::mint debits manager balance, not wallet coin balance directly.
acceptance_criteria: As a trader opening a position, I can fund the exact required DUSDC amount and mint in one wallet signature. As a trader without enough wallet DUSDC, I see the deficit before signing. As a trader, I can distinguish wallet DUSDC balance from manager DUSDC balance and see the post-trade manager balance impact. As a trader, after successful deposit-and-mint, balances and positions refresh without requiring a separate manual deposit step.
close_reason: Implemented inline DUSDC deposit plus mint PTB, wallet funding validation, and post-trade refresh path for oracle open-position preview

title: Add generated Predict bindings and transaction helpers
description: Replace ad hoc raw Move targets for new trading flows with generated or centralized Predict bindings for create_manager, predict_manager::deposit, predict::mint, predict::redeem, and predict::redeem_permissionless. Keep current supply/withdraw helpers working while making transaction construction testable.
acceptance_criteria: As an app developer, I can build manager creation, manager deposit, mint, and redeem PTBs from one typed helper layer. As a trader, mint transactions pass Predict, PredictManager, OracleSVI, MarketKey, quantity, and Clock correctly. As a trader, redeem transactions choose normal or permissionless settled redeem according to oracle state and ownership rules. As a maintainer, helper tests cover MarketKey construction, quantity scaling, and call targets for BTC/DUSDC testnet.
close_reason: Added centralized Predict transaction bindings and tested PTB helpers for manager creation, deposit, mint, redeem, MarketKey construction, DUSDC scaling, and permissionless settled redeem selection.

title: Add PredictManager account discovery and lifecycle
description: Build the wallet-side trading account layer for DeepBook Predict. The app needs to find a user's existing PredictManager from the indexed server, create one on-chain with predict::create_manager when absent, and expose manager id, owner, quote balance, and position-bearing state to trade screens.
acceptance_criteria: As a connected trader, I can see whether I already have a PredictManager. As a connected trader without a manager, I can create one and continue without manually pasting object IDs. As a connected trader, I can see my manager quote balance before opening or closing positions. As a trader, I get a clear empty, loading, error, and server-lag state after manager creation.
close_reason: Implemented wallet PredictManager discovery, create lifecycle, balance and position state UI

title: Decide first-release Predict trading scope
description: Resolve product and protocol assumptions before implementation of DeepBook Predict position open/close flows. Current uncertainty includes whether v1 supports directional positions only or ranges too; whether the app should auto-create a PredictManager; whether it should include manager deposit/withdraw UX or only trade from an existing manager balance; how users obtain DUSDC; whether redeem should support live, settled, and permissionless settled flows; slippage/preview freshness policy; and what portfolio filters are required for BTC/DUSDC.
acceptance_criteria: As a trader, I know v1 supports BTC/DUSDC directional UP/DOWN positions only, with no range support. As a trader, I know the app auto-creates my PredictManager when absent. As a trader opening a position, I know DUSDC deposit and mint happen in one PTB. As a trader closing a position, I know partial and full redeem are supported, and redeemed payout should be withdrawn from the PredictManager in the same PTB when possible. As a trader, I know active trade previews refresh every 5s with a visible countdown, and stale previews are blocked before signing. As a trader, I know DUSDC funding guidance is out of scope.
notes:
Human interview questions: 1) Should v1 support only directional UP/DOWN positions for BTC/DUSDC, or also vertical ranges? 2) Should the app auto-create a PredictManager when absent? 3) Should DUSDC deposits into PredictManager happen inline in the open-position flow, or as a separate wallet/account page? 4) Should close support partial quantities in v1? 5) Should settled positions use owner-only redeem, permissionless redeem, or both? 6) What slippage or stale-preview tolerance is acceptable between preview and signed transaction? 7) Should v1 include DUSDC acquisition/faucet guidance, or assume the wallet is already funded?
Decision outcome: v1 supports BTC/DUSDC directional UP/DOWN only; no ranges. App should auto-create a PredictManager when absent. Opening should compose a single PTB that deposits required DUSDC into the PredictManager and then mints the selected position using that deposited manager balance. Closing should support partial and full redeem; source check confirms predict::redeem accepts quantity and predict_manager::decrease_position only asserts stored quantity >= redeemed quantity. Closing should redeem and withdraw the redeemed payout from PredictManager in the same PTB when possible; use exact settled payout or fresh live preview amount, with stale-preview handling if live payout moves. Settled redeem can use owner redeem first and permissionless as fallback/secondary. During active open/close flow, refresh preview every 5s and show a visual countdown/timer; 5s is acceptable for active trade forms, not for background app-wide polling. DUSDC acquisition/faucet guidance is out of scope for this feature.
close_reason: Recorded v1 product and protocol scope decisions, including directional-only BTC/DUSDC, auto manager creation, same-PTB deposit+mint, partial/full redeem, same-PTB redeem+withdraw target, 5s active preview timer, and DUSDC funding out of scope.

title: make list of beads tasks for open/close DeepBook Predict positions

Each task must have user stories as acceptance criteria

External docs checked: Predict package README at github.com/MystenLabs/deepbookv3/tree/predict-testnet-4-16/packages/predict. Key implementation assumptions captured in child tasks: positions live inside PredictManager, mint debits manager quote balance, redeem pays back into manager, public server is render backend, direct chain reads are needed around wallet writes. bd dolt pull failed because no Dolt remote is configured in this workspace.
close_reason: Created child Beads task breakdown for DeepBook Predict BTC/DUSDC open/close flows and filed human-needed decision bead risk-trading-3ec.1 for remaining requirement gaps.

title: debug why oracle page has high memory usage (11GB!), when you know the root of the problem create a task to fix it
description: this is actually a memory leak because memory grows over time about 1GB per minute
notes: Root cause found in the current oracle route: quote polling and stale timers caused the whole route to re-render, which re-rendered both Recharts charts. Recharts then repeatedly dispatched internal Redux auto-batched chart updates and allocated heap on each refresh. Fix: memoized the chart panels, disabled Recharts line/tooltip animations, and replaced the permanent 1s stale interval with a one-shot stale timeout. Long heap sample stayed flat while quote polling and oracle refreshes continued.
close_reason: Fixed oracle page chart-driven memory growth.

title: Add DeepBook Predict vault liquidity actions
description: Add user-facing supply and withdraw flows for DeepBook Predict vault liquidity. Current app is read-only and only shows oracle, position, and LP activity from predict-server testnet endpoints. Missing pieces: Sui wallet connection, Sui client/network config, package/object/coin type constants, onchain Predict and vault reads, quote coin and PLP balance discovery, transaction builders for predict::supply and predict::withdraw, form validation, previews for vault value/shares/withdrawable amount, transaction signing/execution, post-transaction refresh, error states, and tests/build verification. Docs: https://docs.sui.io/onchain-finance/deepbook-predict/contract-information/predict
acceptance_criteria: Users can connect a Sui wallet on the configured network; users can see accepted quote asset balance, PLP balance, vault value, and currently withdrawable amount; users can submit a supply transaction that deposits quote asset and receives PLP; users can submit a withdraw transaction that burns PLP and receives quote asset; invalid or unavailable amounts are blocked before signing; successful transactions link to explorer and refresh balances/activity; build passes.

Live funded-wallet transaction testing split to risk-trading-0m9.

title: Harden Predict trading edge cases and recovery states
description: Add robust handling for lifecycle, pricing, balance, and indexing edge cases across open and close flows. The goal is to prevent users from signing doomed transactions and make post-submit state understandable when the public server lags the chain.
acceptance_criteria: As a trader, I see disabled actions and specific reasons when an oracle is inactive, expired before settlement, settled, or missing price/SVI data. As a trader, I see clear errors for insufficient manager balance, insufficient wallet DUSDC, insufficient position quantity, invalid strike, invalid quantity, quote asset mismatch, paused trading, and ask bound rejection. As a trader, after submit I can open the transaction in Sui explorer and the UI refreshes direct chain state before relying on indexed server state. As a trader, repeated clicks, wallet rejection, network failure, and server lag do not leave duplicate or misleading pending actions.
close_reason: not important
